commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional context-engine deferred-maintenance debt hint, uses it to skip no-debt background turn maintenance, suppresses no-op completion notices, exports the new params type, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +60, Tests +149. Total +209 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: PR-head source shows a new root SDK export while the PR commit leaves the generated SDK API baseline hash unchanged, and the baseline generator hashes public SDK exports. The no-op maintenance overhead itself is supported by the contributor's live logs, not locally reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow generated contract refresh on the PR branch; an automated repair can run the SDK API generator and leave runtime code untouched.

target.title: feat(context-engine): gate and quiet no-op deferred turn maintenance
target.body:
## Summary

Reduces no-op turn-maintenance overhead from two angles — **scheduling** (skip the deferred pass when the engine reports no durable debt) and **reporting** (suppress the no-op completion notice) — behind one optional, backward-compatible engine hint.

- add optional `ContextEngineInfo.hasDeferredMaintenance(...)` metadata
- skip deferred turn maintenance when an engine explicitly reports no maintenance debt
- keep backward compatibility by scheduling maintenance when the hint is absent or throws
- suppress no-op deferred maintenance completion notices while preserving progress and failures

## What Problem This Solves

`turnMaintenanceMode: "background"` says an engine supports background turn maintenance. It does not necessarily mean durable maintenance debt exists on every turn.

Without a debt hint, the host schedules a deferred maintenance task after successful turns whenever the engine advertises background mode. For engines where most turns have no pending maintenance work, that creates repeated no-op tasks and misleading "background task done" noise.

This adds a small optional host/engine contract seam: engines that can cheaply check durable debt may return `false`; engines that cannot implement the hint continue to get the old behavior.

## Compatibility

- optional API only
- absent hint preserves existing behavior
- throwing hint logs a warning and preserves existing behavior
- real maintenance still runs when the hint returns true
- **Non-goals:** no behavior change for engines without the hint; engine-specific recovery/transplant work is out of scope

## Companion

Lossless Claw implementation: https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/936

## Evidence

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- Regression coverage added for:
  - skipping deferred turn maintenance when `hasDeferredMaintenance(...)` returns `false`
  - preserving current deferred scheduling when the hint is absent
  - preserving failure/progress surfacing while suppressing no-op completion notices

## Proof (real run)

Captured from a live OpenClaw gateway running a companion context engine that implements the hint, before and after deploying this change.

**Before** — on turns with no durable maintenance debt, the host queued a no-op deferred maintenance task on essentially every successful turn:

```
[context-engine] deferred turn maintenance queued taskId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> lane=context-engine-turn-maintenance:<redacted>
```

These fired continuously throughout the day (peaking at ~21 in a single hour; last occurrence immediately before the deploy).

**After** — with the hint live (engine reports no debt → host skips scheduling):

- **0** `deferred turn maintenance queued` lines across the **1,172** turn-level context-engine events logged since the deploy.
- The active conversation had no maintenance-debt row, so `hasDeferredMaintenance(...)` resolved `false` and the host returned without scheduling.

No-op completion notices are likewise suppressed, while real maintenance progress and failures remain visible.
target.author: christian-lallo
